hypothesis: If the Force Delta metric is incorporated as a physics-informed regularization term during fine-tuning of direct-force NNIPs—penalizing the magnitude of U∆alongside the standard energy and force loss—then the resulting models will exhibit not only reduced Force Delta values but also improved generalization to out-of-distribution structures, as measured by lower true DFT force errors on held-out test systems, because explicitly enforcing internal conservative consistency during training will constrain the model to learn a more physically faithful representation of the potential energy surface.
